UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user of the Bhasha Kavach application, storing user-specific settings and preferences, separate from authentication credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification within the application (not for authentication).

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

preferredUiLanguage.description: The user's preferred language for the application interface (e.g., 'en', 'hi').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

TranslationJob.title: TranslationJob
TranslationJob.description: Represents a single translation request or batch of files initiated by a user, tracking its progress and details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TranslationJob entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated this translation job. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N TranslationJob)

originalFileName.description: The original name of the file or content provided by the user.

originalFileType.description: The type of the original input file (e.g., 'docx', 'pdf', 'jpg', 'png', 'txt').

originalContentStoragePath.description: The URI or path to the stored original content in the backend storage.

inputMethod.description: Describes how the input content was provided (e.g., 'file_upload', 'plain_text_input', 'sample_test_input').

sourceLanguage.description: The original language of the input content (e.g., 'en' for English).

targetLanguages.description: An array of target languages requested for translation (e.g., ['hi', 'mr', 'bn']).

status.description: The current processing status of the translation job (e.g., 'PENDING', 'PROCESSING_OCR', 'PROCESSING_TRANSLATION', 'COMPLETED', 'FAILED').

requestedAt.description: Timestamp when the translation job was initiated.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the translation job was completed.

errorMessage.description: Details of any error that occurred during processing, if the status is 'FAILED'.

TranslatedOutput.title: TranslatedOutput
TranslatedOutput.description: Represents a specific translated version of content generated from a TranslationJob for a particular language and format.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TranslatedOutput entity.

translationJobId.description: Reference to the TranslationJob that produced this output. (Relationship: TranslationJob 1:N TranslatedOutput)

targetLanguage.description: The language of this specific translated output (e.g., 'hi', 'mr').

outputFormat.description: The format of this translated output (e.g., 'WORD', 'PDF', 'PLAIN_TEXT', 'PREVIEW_HTML').

outputContentStoragePath.description: The URI or path to the stored translated content file or text in the backend storage.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this translated output was generated.

wordCount.description: The word count of the translated content.

characterCount.description: The character count of the translated content.

Summary.title: Summary
Summary.description: Represents a summarized version of a specific TranslatedOutput, generated by the AI-powered summarization feature.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Summary entity.

translatedOutputId.description: Reference to the TranslatedOutput from which this summary was generated. (Relationship: TranslatedOutput 1:N Summary)

summaryLengthType.description: Describes the intended length or style of the summary (e.g., 'SHORT', 'MEDIUM', 'LONG', 'CUSTOM').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the summary was generated.

definition.description: Stores user-specific settings and preferences, owned by the authenticated user. The document ID for each UserProfile is the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores details of a user's initiated translation job. Includes the 'userId' field (as per schema) and is implicitly owned by the user in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this translation job.

params.name: translationJobId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific translation job.

definition.description: Stores a specific translated version of content generated from a translation job. Will include a denormalized 'userId' field (derived from the path) in the document for authorization independence, alongside the 'translationJobId' as per schema.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent translation job.

params.name: translationJobId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent translation job.

params.name: translatedOutputId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific translated output.

definition.description: Stores a summarized version of a specific translated output. Will include a denormalized 'userId' field (derived from the path) in the document for authorization independence, alongside the 'translatedOutputId' as per schema.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent translated output.

params.name: translationJobId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent translation job.

params.name: translatedOutputId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent translated output.

params.name: summaryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific summary.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following a strict hierarchical, user-owned model, ensuring strong data isolation and simplifying security rules. All core entities (`UserProfile`, `TranslationJob`, `TranslatedOutput`, `Summary`) are nested under the authenticated user's path (`/users/{userId}/...`), making user ownership explicit and easily enforceable.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This structure explicitly prioritizes authorization independence. Each document within the user's hierarchy (i.e., `TranslationJob`, `TranslatedOutput`, and `Summary`) will include the `userId` (denormalized from the path or the original `UserProfile`'s ID) directly within its document data. This eliminates the need for `get()` operations in security rules to check parent document ownership. For instance, to verify access to a `TranslatedOutput` document, a rule can simply check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without needing to fetch its parent `TranslationJob` or the grandparent `UserProfile`. This approach is crucial for enabling atomic operations and making rules highly debuggable and performant.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The hierarchical path combined with denormalized `userId` directly supports Query-Allow-Permissions (QAPs) by ensuring that `list` operations are inherently secure. When a user queries `/users/{request.auth.uid}/translationJobs`, for example, all documents returned by the query will, by virtue of the path, belong to that user. The security rules will then simply validate `request.auth.uid == userId` in the path. There is no need for client-side filtering or server-side `where` clauses on `userId` that rules would then need to validate using non-indexed lookups or complex `get()` calls. This design allows for efficient and secure listing of user-specific data, as all documents in a given collection (e.g., `translationJobs`) for a specific user belong to that user.

**Structural Segregation:** Each collection (`userProfiles`, `translationJobs`, `translatedOutputs`, `summaries`) maintains a homogeneous security posture: all documents within these user-specific subcollections are owned by the `userId` defined in the parent path. This avoids mixing data with different access requirements, further simplifying security rule logic.

**Access Modeling:** The design consistently uses Path-Based ownership (`/users/{userId}/...`) for all user-specific and user-owned data. This is the most secure and straightforward pattern for managing private user data and its related entities.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:** Wildcards like `{userId}`, `{translationJobId}`, `{translatedOutputId}`, and `{summaryId}` are descriptive and semantic, enhancing readability and maintainability of both the data structure and the security rules.
